content: We can conceptualize every autonomous agent as a localized originator of fan effect propagation, where the agent’s exerted power and causal effects spread outward in ever-widening, attenuating ripples through social, biological, or computational networks, just as a fan’s influence spreads across a cultural system. This framework allows us to quantify an agent’s real-world impact by measuring how its causal effects decay as they propagate across network edges, rather than only measuring its inherent capabilities.
